rulings.Case Title: SHARON WOODY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Pursuant To Cal. Code Civ. Pro. § 583.410 And Supporting Papers
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JANUARY 10, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Canoga Builders Supplies¿ motion to dismiss for delay in prosecution pursuant to C.C.P. 583.410 and 583.420(a)(2)(B) is granted. No opposition filed. Hearing required unless counsel for defendant informs plaintiffs of this tentative ruling and they state that they do not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIE HOWARD VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JANUARY 10, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2, Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff¿s claim for punitive damages is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD SHORTALL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JANUARY 10, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3, Defendant Golden Gate Drywall, Inc.¿s motion to dismiss for delay in prosecution pursuant to C.C.P. 583.410 and 583.420(a)(1) and CRC 3.1342 is denied. Although plaintiff failed to serve defendant within two years of commencing this action, plaintiff presented a credible excuse for the delay. Further, the court does not find prejudice to defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND PANGBURN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JANUARY 10, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 4, Defendant Eaton Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is off calendar. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of production. Based on defendant¿s moving papers, defendant attempted to shift its burden via the negation of an element prong of Aguilar. Defendant¿s moving papers failed to demonstrate that plaintiff did not perform any activities to a Cutler-Hammer product that would have caused the release of respirable asbestos. Plaintiff¿s objections to Mr. Rabinowitz¿s declaration are sustained. Plaintiff has dismissed his claim for punitive damages, which was the only issue defendant sought to have summarily adjudicated. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DORIS HENDERSON et al VS. METROPOLITAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Amendment To Complaint To Substitute Defendants True Name For Does 1 1001
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JANUARY 10, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 5, Defendant A. Teichert & Son, Inc.¿s motion to strike amendment to complaint to substitute defendant¿s true name for Does 1, 1001 is off calendar. Defendant was dismissed December 22. = (503/TLJ)
